days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the Coastal City

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Touch down at Hong Kong International Airport. Proceed to immigration and luggage claim before heading to the city via the Airport Express.

timeline.title: Check in to The Burlington Hotel
timeline.detail: Check into this stylish hotel located just a short walk from the MTR station. Take a moment to freshen up and relax before exploring.

timeline.title: Visit Victoria Harbour
timeline.detail: Stroll along the scenic waterfront promenade of Victoria Harbour, taking in breathtaking views of the skyline. This is a great spot for sunset photography.

timeline.title: Dinner at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Savor authentic wonton noodles at this famous eatery known for its chewy noodles and delicious broth. Expect queues, but it's worth the wait!

timeline.title: Avenue of Stars Visit
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely walk along the Avenue of Stars, a tribute to Hong Kong's film industry. Enjoy night views of the harbour and take plenty of photos of the skyline.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: Head back to your hotel after a delightful first day in Hong Kong. Prepare for an exciting second day!

localTips: Purchase an Octopus card at the airport for hassle-free travel on public transport.
Consider a HK SIM card to stay connected during your trip.
Some restaurants in Hong Kong do not accept credit cards; cash is preferred.

days.title: Cultural Exploration in Kowloon
days.subtitle: Food, History, and Local Life

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Indulge in a traditional Hong Kong-style breakfast of milk tea, toast, and scrambled eggs at this local favorite eatery, known for speedy service.

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Explore Wong Tai Sin Temple, a popular shrine that attracts many worshippers. Experience the traditional rituals and the vibrancy of local spirituality.

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Enjoy dim sum at the world's most affordable Michelin-starred restaurant. Don't miss the baked buns with BBQ pork, a signature dish.

timeline.title: Explore Mong Kok Street Markets
timeline.detail: Spend the afternoon wandering through the vibrant street markets of Mong Kok. Visit the Ladies' Market for souvenirs and the Goldfish Market for unique finds.

timeline.title: Dinner at Temple Street Night Market
timeline.detail: Enjoy local street food at Temple Street Night Market, where you can feast on everything from skewers to sweet desserts while soaking up the lively atmosphere.

timeline.title: Kowloon Night Market Experience
timeline.detail: Continue exploring the night market, enjoying the street performances and trying more local treats. Don't forget to haggle for souvenirs!

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: End your exploratory day with a relaxing walk back to your hotel, reflecting on the experiences of the day.

highlights: Dim sum at Tim Ho Wan
localTips: Get to Tim Ho Wan early to avoid long lines during peak hours.
Carry an umbrella; Kowloon can experience sudden rain.
Bargain at street markets; it's expected and part of the fun.

days.title: Lantau Island Adventure
days.subtitle: Nature and Scenic Views

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a relaxed breakfast at the hotel before heading out for your Lantau Island adventure. Try some local pastries alongside coffee.
timeline.mapQuery: The Burlington Hotel Cafe, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Take the MTR to Tung Chung
timeline.detail: Catch the MTR from your nearest station to Tung Chung, the gateway to Lantau Island. Enjoy the smooth and quick ride.
timeline.mapQuery: MTR Tung Chung Station, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car Ride
timeline.detail: Experience a stunning cable car ride to Ngong Ping, where you will witness breathtaking views of Lantau Island's natural beauty from above.

timeline.title: Visit the Tian Tan Buddha
timeline.detail: Walk to the majestic Tian Tan Buddha statue and marvel at this cultural landmark. Climb the stairway for a panoramic view of the island.

timeline.title: Lunch at Po Lin Monastery
timeline.detail: Savor a vegetarian meal at Po Lin Monastery, known for its wholesome dishes. It's a serene setting to enjoy traditional Buddhist cuisine.

timeline.title: Explore Lantau Island Trails
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely hike along the beautiful trails of Lantau Island, surrounded by lush greenery and coastal views. It's a tranquil escape from the city.

timeline.title: Return to Tung Chung
timeline.detail: Head back down via the cable car for another view of the scenic island. Prepare for your return trip to the city.

timeline.title: Dinner at Shopping Mall near Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a final dinner at a nearby shopping mall that features diverse dining options before you wind down after your exciting trip.
timeline.mapQuery: Shopping Mall near The Burlington Hotel, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Prepare for Departure
timeline.detail: Pack your bags and prepare for departure tomorrow. Make sure all travel documents and souvenirs are secure for your trip back home.

highlights: Cable car ride over Lantau Island
localTips: Book the Ngong Ping 360 tickets in advance to skip the queues.
Wear comfortable shoes for hiking around Lantau Island.
Check the weather before embarking on Lantau Island as it can change quickly.

hotels.name: The Burlington Hotel
hotels.note: 5-min walk from Times Square, centrally located in Causeway Bay.
